video_title: Top 5 #DDD Videos in Minnesota with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Soul Avenue Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Moorhead, Minnesota. This is Soul Avenue Kitchen. Soul Avenue is global street food with a Midwest interpretation. What they brought to the community is not your standard run-of-the-mill stuff.

dishes.dish_name: Chicken Fried Chicken Fried Fried Chicken Fried Sandwich
dishes.description: Boneless chicken thighs marinated overnight in egg, buttermilk, flour, pepper, salt, and pot sauce. Double dredged in seasoned flour (salt, black pepper, white pepper, cayenne, smoked paprika, onion powder, garlic powder, baking powder) and double fried. Served on a toasted bun with lime aioli, kimchi, som jang, and house pickles.
dishes.guy_response: My friend, that is an excellent fried chicken. That is super crunchentatious. I have a chicken joint called Chicken Guy, and I say that sauce is the boss. When I watch you put on all that som jang, I'm like... I'm gonna call it the som jang sandwich. But it's this balance that goes on between the fat of that lime aioli with the acid of the pickle. It's like clashing right there in the middle of the chicken sandwich. Ba-boom. Dude, outstanding.

restaurants.name: Rustica Eatery and Tavern
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here to check out a joint where they've got a great story. This is Rustica Eatery and Tavern. The building was originally built in 1898. But now it's all about fresh takes on classic dishes.

dishes.description: Chuck's shoulder beef seasoned, seared, then braised for at least four hours with oregano, thyme, bay leaf, mirepoix, garlic, salt, tomato guts, red wine, chicken stock, and red chili flake. The jus is strained and reduced. Served over crispy fried fingerling potatoes with Mahon cheese, parsley, bacon, roasted garlic, and a side of beef gravy.
dishes.guy_response: This right here, I don't even know if calling it poutine is the right thing to do because it's a very elevated poutine. Going to a regular french fry potato would have been simple. You take it and mix it up, get it, and throw in the fingerlings, which is the royalty of potatoes. And then you make a gravy that you really take some time and energy to do. This dish has got a lot more variation than you would find in most poutines. A little diamond. Poutine, head in the window. The meat is rich. It's tender. Gravy brings me back to home.

restaurants.name: Andale
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Richfield, Minnesota, about 10 minutes from downtown Minneapolis. Now, this is a great place to come to. One, a bunch of my Triple D crew lives here. Two, they have got an eclectic mix of restaurants. But I got to be honest with you, when my crew tells me they've got a real deal Mexican joint in Minnesota, you know I got to check it out. This is Andale. You can't get this kind of authentic Mexican anywhere in the Twin Cities. It's like walking into a little cafe in Mexico. And much more, because when Fernando and Jorge Rangel opened in 2011, they went for, well, pardon the expression, the whole enchilada. One side is full blown restaurant. Right. And a huge menu. The other side is market. You got a butcher shop, you have a bakery.

dishes.description: Pork marinated for 24 hours in a sauce made from guajillo pepper, orange juice, lime juice, cumin, black pepper, bay leaves, oregano, aciote (annatto seed paste), garlic, onions, salt, and white vinegar. Cooked on a flat top with onions, bacon, and pineapple. Served on double tortillas with cilantro, onions, lime juice, and avocado salsa verde.
dishes.guy_response: There is no way we're in Minnesota. No way. The meat perfectly marinated. It's got a nice crisp to it. Right amount of onion, tortillas are charred. The salsa is outstanding.

restaurants.name: George and the Dragon
restaurants.guy_intro: So what do you call a joint that is English pub with a little Filipino flair? You're right. This is George and the Dragon. Something different and unique, yet down to earth and in the neighborhood. This is like the cheers of South Minneapolis. Filipino flair on an English pub. Yeah. Something you hear every day. Every day here since 2011, when this joint was opened by the Navarro family. Fred, Stacey and Paco.

dishes.description: Onion jam (caramelized onions with brown sugar, balsamic vinegar, and water) mixed with whipped cream cheese, mayonnaise, Worcestershire sauce, salt, cayenne pepper, Coleman's dry mustard, and Parmesan cheese. Topped with cooked cold bacon and more Parmesan, then baked at 350 degrees for five minutes. Finished with chives.
dishes.guy_response: That's ridiculous. You know what? This should be illegal. It's not just onions and bacon. There's much more depth. I think you know what really makes it? Is that mustard. A little tang of that. Very good. Excellent. Nice job.

restaurants.name: Pizzeria Lola
restaurants.guy_intro: Did you know they have one of the only copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza ovens, a Le Pagnole, at this place where they're doing off-the-hook pizza? This is Pizzeria Lola. A lot of unusual flavors. Sliced and served up by owner Ann Kim, who grew up learning to cook from her Korean grandma before discovering another passion a little later in life.

dishes.description: Pizza made with a 48-hour fermented dough (poolish method). Topped with house red sauce (California tomatoes, red pear tomatoes, puree, salt, dried basil, dried oregano, granulated garlic, onion powder), Wisconsin mozzarella, house-made fennel sausage (ground Berkshire pork, toasted fennel seeds, fresh chopped garlic, chili flake, salt, pepper), and natural casing pepperoni. Cooked in a copper-covered, wood-fired French pizza oven (Le Pagnole) at 550-600 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: We got this heirloom pork with the real fat in the real flavor and the fennels rocking this. A lot of people say, oh, sausage and pepperoni. I've had that all the time. This is way beyond your everyday pizza joint on the corner. Sausage and pepperoni. You got to try this one. It's the most heightened, delicious pepperoni sausage pizza you've ever had. You're like, this is what pepperoni is supposed to look like.
